data.title: Higher oil prices keep heightening the risks that inflation will damage the economy – analyst
data.description:
Rising oil prices (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM) driven by ongoing geopolitical tensions with Iran are heightening the risks that inflation will damage the economy, according to Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen of Main Street Alpha.

"Another day, another Iran-headline-dominated day," Nelissen said. "As long as oil (CO1:COM) keeps rising based on supply worries caused by a potentially prolonged war, the higher the risks are that inflation damages the economy, including through rising rates."

The analyst noted that market volatility is being compounded by accelerating artificial intelligence capital expenditure spending among major technology companies.

Nelissen warned that AI CapEx spending is rising faster than anticipated, which is hurting market-leading Big Tech stocks, particularly the hyperscalers.

He characterized the current environment as "a tricky part of the market" and does not expect any calmness until the Iran situation is settled.

However, Ross Gerber, president and CEO of Gerber Kawasaki Wealth and Investment Management, offered a more optimistic perspective on the spending surge. "Capex is not bad. It's good," Gerber said.

"These companies have been printing money for years. They see the huge AI opportunity and are investing to control this future and its profits. This is what good businesses do."

Mohamed El-Erian, chief economic adviser at Allianz, highlighted the broad market impact of rising energy prices.

He noted that stocks (SP500), (COMP:IND), (DJI), bonds (US10Y), (US2Y), (BND), (SPLB), crypto (BTC-USD), (ETH-USD), and gold (XAUUSD:CUR) are all declining, with energy (XLE) as the notable exception.

El-Erian warned that $100 Brent crude (CO1:COM) complicates the economic, corporate, and policy outlooks, urging investors to keep a close eye on debt and leverage dynamics.

data.title: Boyd Gaming sees growth as Midwest and South properties offset Las Vegas weakness
data.description: Boyd Gaming (BYD) reported revenue rose 4.0% year over year to $1.04B in the casino company's second quarter. Net income was $131.2M vs. $151.5M a year ago. Total adjusted EBITDAR was $350.5M vs. $357.9M a year ago. Non-GAAP EPS of $1.93 was ahead of the consensus estimate of $1.89 and below last year's mark of $1.87.The company noted that its Midwest & South segment once again delivered revenue and adjusted EBITDAR growth during the quarter, driven by increased play from our core and retail customers, as well as contributions from recent capital investments across the segment. However, results in the Las Vegas Locals segment were impacted by continued softness in destination business, primarily at the Orleans, and ongoing construction disruption at the Suncoast. The Downtown Las Vegas segment was negatively impacted by ongoing softness in destination business throughout the downtown area but did see play from core and Hawaiian customers consistent with recent quarters.Boyd Gaming repurchased $156M in shares during the second quarter and had $551M remaining under its current share repurchase authorization as of June 30. At the end of the quarter, Boyd Gaming (BYD) had a cash position of $323M and total debt of $2.6B."Our second-quarter results demonstrated the benefits of our diversified business model, with strong performances from our Midwest & South operations, online segment, and managed business. Results for the quarter, on a comparable basis, reflect both revenue and adjusted EBITDAR growth, with property operating margins of 40%, a level we have consistently delivered over the last several years," highlighted CEO Keith Smith.Smith said with Boyd's (BYD) strong balance sheet, efficient operating model, and robust free cash flow, the casino company is well-positioned to continue creating long-term shareholder value.Shares of Boyd Gaming (BYD) were inactive in the postmarket session. The casino stock has traded in a relatively narrow range of $85 to $90 over the last six weeks.

data.title: Stocks to watch on Thursday after hours: INTC, RNG, and HIG
data.description:
U.S. equities concluded deep in the red on Thursday as Middle East tensions pushed oil prices above $100 a barrel.

The S&P 500 (SP500) fell -1.2%, the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) dropped -2.1%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) declined -0.9%.

Now, here are the three stocks to watch on Thursday after hours:

  • Shares of Intel (INTC) surged 11.5% after the bell after the company posted better-than-expected second-quarter results. Non-GAAP EPS came in at $0.42, beating estimates by $0.20. At the same time, revenue reached $16.1 billion, up 24.8% year over year and $1.65 billion above expectations.
  • Shares of RingCentral (RNG) jumped 3.1% in after-hours trading on the back of stronger-than-expected second-quarter results. Non-GAAP EPS came in at $1.22, beating estimates by $0.05. Revenue totaled $657 million, up 5.9% year over year and $6.44 million above consensus.
  • Shares of Hartford Insurance Group (HIG) fell 1% after the bell on Thursday even as the insurer delivered better-than-expected second-quarter results. Non-GAAP EPS came in at $3.42, beating estimates by $0.28. Revenue reached $7.26 billion, up 3.9% year over year and $20 million above consensus.

data.title: RingCentral Non-GAAP EPS of $1.22 beats by $0.05, revenue of $657M beats by $6.44M
data.description:
• RingCentral press release (RNG): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.22 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $657M (+5.9% Y/Y) beats by $6.44M.

  • Financial Outlook

    Third Quarter 2026 Guidance:

    • Subscriptions revenue of $643 to $649 million.
    • Total revenue of $664 to $670 million vs. consensus revenue of $663.55M
    • GAAP operating margin of 7.2% to 8.6%.
    • Non-GAAP operating margin of 23.5% to 24.0%
    • Non-GAAP EPS of $1.25 to $1.30  vs. consensus EPS of $1.26 based on approximately 86.5 million fully diluted shares.
    • Share-based compensation of $63 to $67 million.

    Our full year 2026 guidance is:

    • Raising subscriptions revenue range to $2.550 billion to $2.561 billion.
    • Raising total revenue range to $2.635 billion to $2.646 billion vs. consensus revenue of $2.63B
    • Raising GAAP operating margin to 9.0% to 9.7%.
    • Raising non-GAAP operating margin to approximately 23.6% to 24.0%.
    • Raising non-GAAP EPS of $4.96 to $5.10  vs. consensus EPS of $4.95 based on 87.0 to 86.5 million fully diluted shares.
    • Share-based compensation of $240 to $245 million.
    • Raising free cash flow guidance of $615 to $625 million.

data.title: Digital Realty Trust FFO of $2.13 beats by $0.15, revenue of $1.92B beats by $260M
data.description:
• Digital Realty Trust press release (DLR): Q2 FFO of $2.13 beats by $0.15.
  • Revenue of $1.92B (+28.9% Y/Y) beats by $260M.
  • Signed total bookings during 2Q26 that are expected to generate $307 million of annualized GAAP base rent at 100% share; at Digital Realty’s share, bookings were $208 million, including a $108 million contribution from the 0-1 megawatt plus interconnection category
  • Reported a record total backlog of $1.9 billion of annualized GAAP base rent at 100% share, at the end of 2Q26; at Digital Realty’s share, the backlog was $1.4 billion
  • Raised 2026 Core FFO per share (excluding net promote) outlook to $8.15 - $8.20 vs $8.04 consensus and 2026 Constant-Currency Core FFO per share (excluding net promote) outlook to $8.10 - $8.15

data.title: Boyd Gaming Non-GAAP EPS of $1.93 beats by $0.04, revenue of $1.04B in-line
data.description:
• Boyd Gaming press release (BYD): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.93 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $1.04B (+4.0% Y/Y) in-line.

  • As of June 30, 2026, Boyd Gaming had cash on hand of $322.7 million, and total debt of $2.6 billion. 

  • Boyd Gaming paid a quarterly cash dividend of $0.20 per share on July 15, 2026, as previously announced.

data.title: Mattel expands WWE lineup with Lucha Libra action figures
data.description:
• Mattel (MAT) has expanded its World Wrestling Entertainment ((WWE)) (TKO) portfolio with Mexico’s Lucha Libre to launch a new line of AAA action figures inspired by the promotion’s legendary stars.
  • Beginning in the fall of 2027, Mattel’s (MAT) Lucha Libre AAA collection will expand on its WWE Elite Collection with AAA’s most recognizable talent, including Psycho Clown, El Hijo del Viking, La Parka, and Mr. Iguana.
  • To commemorate the announcement, Mattel Creations will debut a made-to-order Mr. Iguana action figure at San Diego Comic-Con, marking the first product in the new AAA collection.
  • In August 2025, WWE acquired AAA in partnership with Mexico-based sports and entertainment holding company, Fillip.

data.title: SkyWest GAAP EPS of $2.54 misses by $0.24, revenue of $1.1B misses by $20M
data.description:

  • SkyWest secured an agreement to purchase and operate 11 new E175 aircraft for American Airlines
  • SkyWest’s Board of Directors approved a $250 million increase to its existing stock repurchase program

data.title: Winpak Ltd. GAAP EPS of $0.57, revenue of $294.4M
data.description:
• Winpak Ltd. press release (WPK:CA): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.57.
  • Revenue of $294.4M.
  • Net income attributable to equity holders of the Company (Earnings) for the second quarter of 2026 of $33.6 million advanced by 11.3 percent from the $30.2 million recorded in the corresponding quarter in 2025. The improvement in gross profit was a key factor, raising Earnings by $6.0 million. Conversely, foreign exchange lowered Earnings by $1.3 million. Furthermore, operating expenses subtracted $1.0 million from Earnings. In combination, all other factors decreased Earnings by $0.3 million.
